sections.paragraph:
μεστός, ή, όν (s. μεστόω; Trag., X., Pla.+) prim. ‘full’.

① pert. to filling up a space, full, lit., w. gen. of thing (X., An. 1, 4, 19; Alciphron 2, 11; Jos., Ant. 4, 93; PGrenf I, 14, 9; POxy 1070, 31f [III a.d.]) σκεῦος ὄξους μ. full of vinegar J 19:29a. Likew. of a sponge μ. τοῦ ὄξους vs. 29b. τὸ δίκτυον μ. ἰχθύων μεγάλων the net full of large fish 21:11. In imagery of the tongue μ. ἰοῦ full of poison Js 3:8.

② pert. to being thoroughly characterized by someth., filled w. somet., fig. ext. of 1, w. gen.

ⓐ of pers. (Dio Chrys. 51 [68], 4; Ael. Aristid. 46 p. 267 D.: ὕβρεων κ. κακῶν μ.; 47 p. 435 εὐλαβείας; CPR I, 19, 15 μ. ψευδολογίας; POxy 130, 6 μ. ἐλεημοσύνης; Pr 6:34; Jos., Ant. 16, 351) μ. ὑποκρίσεως καὶ ἀνομίας full of pretense and lawlessness Mt 23:28. μ. φθόνου (Maximus Tyr. 35, 4e; Tetrast. Iamb. 1, 31, 2 p. 276) Ro 1:29. μ. πολλῆς ἀνοίας καὶ πονηρίας 2 Cl 13:1 (Isocr. 5, 45 πολλῆς ἀνοίας μ.; Dio Chrys. 15 [32], 15 μ. πονηρίας). μ. ἀγαθωσύνης Ro 15:14. μ. ἐλέους Js 3:17 (plus μεστὴ καρπῶν ἀγαθῶν P74). μ. ὁσίας βουλῆς 1 Cl 2:3.

ⓑ of things (Epicurus in Diog. L. 10, 146 πάντα ταραχῆς μεστά; Menand., Fgm. 386 Kö.; μεστόν ἐστι τὸ ζῆν φροντίδων; Philo, Op. M. 2; 22 al.; Just., D. 131 ὀνείδους μεστοῦ μυστηρίου τοῦ σταυροῦ; Tat. 32, 3 φθόνου μεστά) ὀφθαλμοὶ μ. μοιχαλίδος (s. μοιχαλίς a; μ. w. an abstr. noun Sb VI, 9622, 16 [343 a.d.] ἦθος … ἀπονοίας μεστὸν ἀναλαβόμενοι=displaying a manner fraught with rebellion) 2 Pt 2:14. The way of death is κατάρας μ. 20:1; D 5:1.—B. 931. DELG. M-M. TW.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

prim. prim. = primarily

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

gen. gen. = genitive

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PGrenf I PGrenf I = BGrenfell, An Alexandrian Erotic Fragment, etc.—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

someth. someth. = something

somet. somet. = sometimes

ext. ext. = extension

pers. pers. = person(s)

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

CPR CPR = Corpus Papyrorum Raineri—List 4

Maximus Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

abstr. abstr. = abstractum pro concreto (abstract for the concrete)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 635
